reasoning: All parameters hold steady within target range; 24-hour trends show minor upward drift in pH, TDS, and temp—expected post-water-change and entirely normal for a young, maturing tank. Toby is on top of calibration, duckweed is thriving, and shrimp are actively feeding and visible—colony acclimating well.

actions.note: Monitor duckweed growth density over next few days; quantification plan is good—track if surface coverage impacts gas exchange or lower plant light.
After probe calibration tomorrow, re-baseline pH and TDS to confirm current drift is sensor drift, not genuine water chemistry shift.
Calibrate pH probe as planned; will sharpen confidence in trend interpretation.
Continue watching competitive feeding behavior—healthy sign of appetite and social dominance settling; note any appetite changes as early stress indicator.
